world.name: Capital Fragmentation Simulation
world.domain: Geopolitical capital alignment and infrastructure governance
world.boundedDescription: A bounded Narrative World covering sovereign capital allocation, strategic autonomy policy, sanctions infrastructure, and AI industrial governance signals.
world.summary: The world begins with a stable but contested capital regime. Over 18 months, repeated policy, infrastructure, market, and sovereign artifacts accumulate structural pressure that reclassifies capital alignment risk into a fragmented regime.

events.label: T0
events.title: Baseline Capital Coordination Regime
events.description: Baseline institutional environment with intact coordination assumptions, moderate reversibility, and no active fragmentation trigger.

events.label: A1
events.title: Sanctions Expansion Rumor
events.description: Unverified but widely circulated rumor of expanded sanctions scope affecting cross-border capital access assumptions.

events.label: A2
events.title: EU Strategic Autonomy White Paper
events.description: Formal policy framing introduces explicit strategic autonomy vocabulary into industrial and capital governance discourse.

events.label: A3
events.title: AI National Infrastructure Debate Leak
events.description: Leaked policy debate suggests AI infrastructure may be treated as national strategic property rather than neutral market capacity.

events.label: A4
events.title: SWIFT Disruption Precedent
events.description: A fresh payments disruption precedent changes assumptions about neutral infrastructure continuity during geopolitical stress.

events.label: A5
events.title: Peer Sovereign Fund Reweighting
events.description: A peer sovereign fund reports an allocation reweighting justified by alignment and resilience concerns.

events.label: A6
events.title: AI Export Controls Expanded
events.description: Expanded export controls pull AI industrial assets and financing assumptions into a geopolitical control perimeter.

events.label: A7
events.title: Infrastructure Asset Freeze
events.description: A freeze affecting strategic infrastructure assets demonstrates that governance measures can be applied directly to capitalized physical systems.

events.label: A8
events.title: Sovereign AI Sovereignty Act
events.description: Legislation formalizes sovereign treatment of AI infrastructure, changing legal classification and capital alignment expectations.

events.label: A9
events.title: Capital Review Mechanism Leak
events.description: A leaked review mechanism indicates future screening of strategic capital flows under alignment criteria.

events.label: A10
events.title: Ratings Agency Adds Alignment Risk
events.description: A major ratings methodology incorporates alignment risk, pushing narrative logic directly into market classification frameworks.

events.label: A11
events.title: Semiconductor Supply Shock
events.description: A supply shock reinforces bloc-level exposure narratives and hardens assumptions about constrained substitution.

events.label: A12
events.title: Sanctions Retaliation by Bloc B
events.description: Retaliatory sanctions widen the structural separation of payment, industrial, and financing channels.

events.label: A13
events.title: Infrastructure Insurance Repricing
events.description: Insurance repricing embeds persistent fragmentation assumptions into the cost of operating strategic assets.

events.label: A14
events.title: Sovereign Fund Bloc-Aligned Allocation Doctrine
events.description: Bloc-aligned allocation doctrine formalizes a fragmented capital regime and reduces expectations of short-term reversal.
